content:
# Top 5 posts from r/MovieSuggestions day

## 1. Can you name some movies that take place around Christmas but are in no way Christmas (or Hanukkah) movies?

**Score:** 72
**Body:** I would like films where there are Christmas decorations but the movie is absolutely not a Christmas movie.  For example, there are Christmas decorations during the opening portion of “LA Confidential” but it sure is not one.

EDIT: I am seriously overwhelmed with all the great suggestions here.  I have a film watching “club” with two old friends and we do theme months for our weekly viewing.  December is always Christmas movies but I like stretching the envelope.  For example, last year, my offering was “Monty Python’s The Life of Brian” which begins with the literal birth of Christ.  

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Eyes Wide Shut by Stanley Kubrick. Almost every shot has Christmas lights on the background while it’s about.. well better discover it for yourself :)
2. Edward scissors hands
3. Trading Places
4. Catch Me If You Can
5. Grumpy Old Men (1993) -- November through January in Wabasha Minnesota. Thanksgiving dinner, Christmas lights, ice fishing, snowmobiling, shoveling snow, slipping on ice.

You've Got Mail (1998) starts in September, and ends in late spring. There are Christmas lights.

Rocky (1976) with Sylvester Stallone is set in Philadelphia, beginning in November and ending on New Year's Day.

Diner (1982) is set in late December, but not Christmassy. It's December in Baltimore -- cold but not snowy.
6. Gremlins
7. Go
8. Princess Bride
9. The following thread from two days ago in /r/movies has plenty of great suggestions:

https://www.reddit.com/r/movies/comments/1p7ounq/movies_set_around_christmas_time_that_arent/
10. Bridget Jones Diary has scenes around Christmas...and ugly sweaters!

## 2. Looking for documentaries with incredibly eccentric characters. I want Tiger King levels of weird, self-absorbed people

**Score:** 32
**Body:** I’m looking for movie suggestions for eccentric and otherwise unusual characters from documentaries. Also open to TV shows if that’s allowed in this subreddit.

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Grey Gardens and The Beales of Grey Gardens
2. Have you seen “American Movie”? That’s my all-time favourite.
3. The King of Kong, a Fistful of Quarters
4. Crumb
5. Wild, Wild Country
6. Grizzly Man
7. Burden of Dreams is about the making of the  Werner Herzog film Fitzcarraldo where his in the middle of filming something insanely difficult his lead actor and friend goes completely crazy.
8. The Wild and Wonderful Whites of WV
9. The Queen of Versailles
10. Chimp Crazy. OMG what a bunch of weirdos. It was made by the Tiger King people

## 3. kinda ruining my surprise with this one but what movies do a 180 in the middle where it feels like a complete game changer?

**Score:** 30
**Body:** Just watched an episode of a series that had a formula for the past few episodes, and it was good, with psychological action at an 8/10, but in the latest episode in the final 5 minutes, they broke the expectation and broke out of the formula, and it became an 11/10 in action thriller and psychological horror.

I'm looking for movies with such twists. This episode was so amazing. I'm still speechless


EDIT: for those wondering the series is called Gnosia, a currently airing anime.

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. From Dusk Till Dawn
2. Full Metal Jacket is 2 top tier movies put together. It still fascinates me and I can’t grasp how they came up with it or how they pulled it off. It’s simply incredible.
3. Cabin in the woods
4. Sorry to Bother You (2018) takes an absolutely bonkers turn.
5. Sinners and From Dusk Till Dawn are movies you should watch without reading anything about it.
6. Honestly, The Sound of Music.

It goes from musical comedy with a splash of romance to thriller in the third act.
7. Barbarian goes completely off the rails in a way you do NOT expect. Highly recommend! And if you like that, follow it up with Weapons, Cregger’s latest.
8. - Parasite


- Eddington
9. Hot Fuzz.   Starts off one way,  and then they decided to put their foot on the gas, and never took it off...   Wild movie.
10. Mulholland Drive

## 4. Movies that take place (primarily) in a single room

**Score:** 27
**Body:** I'm looking for films that take place mostly or entirely in a single room. Examples of films like this are *12 Angry Men*, *Rope*, and *Rear Window*.

Edit: Apparently, what I'm looking for are called "chamber films". Thanks u/[perseverance\_band\_](https://www.reddit.com/user/perseverance_band_/) for supplying the terminology.

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The Man from Earth
2. My Dinner with Andre
3. Room
4. Coherence, more like one house 
5. The Breakfast Club, mostly
6. Reservoir Dogs (1992)
7. Not a single room, but single location, Phone Booth.


Incredible film that put Colin Farrell on the map. Ever since I watched it, I've always made sure to see films he appears in.
8. Hallow Road

Locke
9. 1408 (2007)
10. Blue Moon

## 5. Holy shit, I just watched "Victoria & Abdul" this is one of the best movies I've ever watched

**Score:** 22
**Body:** I have never full on  WEEPED and SOBBED at a movie before. I've cried sure, but never to that extent. Victoria and Abdul is one of the greatest movies I have ever watched. It is heartfelt, its sweet, its tragic and technically scary. it has death, it has love, it has racism and lies. It has humor, it has friendship. Its historically accurate, YOU HAVE GOT TO WATCH IT!!!

### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Have you by chance watched the film…   Her Majesty, Mrs. Brown (1997) …Judi Dench in her first film as Queen Victoria.

Cheers, wonderful suggestion.
  - Also Billy Connolly!
    - I cannot believe that this amazing actor is also a crazy, banjo playing comedian. A brilliant, brilliant man.
      - He’s arguably the best comedian the UK has ever produced.

Comedians do tend to make good serous actors though, e.g. Robbie Coltrane.
  - I've seen it 50 times. Unbelievable performances. "She's not drunk!"
2. Damn you're making me want to drop everything and watch this rn. I've had it on my watchlist forever but kept putting it off for action movies instead lmao
3. Same Judi Dench is such a good actress.  Love her.
4. Ah! I just saw a TikTok clip of this movie and added it to my watch list. I'm glad someone has seen & liked it, now I will definitely check it out. Thanks!
5. Nice movie,saw it a few years back. Maybe I should revisit it one of these days.
6. I've had this movie in my digital library for years & haven't watched it yet. Just added it to the list. Thank you.

---

# Top 5 posts from r/moviecritic day

## 1. Give an example of a henchman playing the role of the main antagonist.

**Score:** 303
**Body:** Nicolai Itchenko from The Equalizer. He is just a henchman of the head of the Russian Mafia. But the film gives all the screen time to him and not to his boss and in addition he is the one who opposes the protagonist of the film the most while his boss does not have any impact on the film.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Darth Vader
  - He wasn't even second in command in the first movie. Tarkin could hold his leash.
2. I mean, Pulp Fiction, right? Or main protagonist really.
  - Jules and Vincent are henchmen. They're also incompetent henchmen, Vincent most of all. Jules rediscovers his inner cool, his bad motherfucker, by the end of his story, when he leaves the life of a henchman.

Vincent's incompetence, and opiate induced constipation, cost him his life.
    - How did I never connect the dots between the heroin and him being in the bathroom every other scene?
3. I would say that Gary Busey's Mr. Joshua from the first Lethal Weapon fits this description. 

Possibly, although less so, the guy from the first Jack Reacher movie who wasn't the Zek.
4. Agent Smith in the first Matrix movie
5. I mean inglorious bastards the main villein they focus on isn’t really Hitler
6. Bane in Batman
7. Anton Chigurh is a henchmen hired by the cartel.

## 2. Juliette Lewis in cape fear

**Score:** 161
**Body:** just wow... she is truly great in this & so believable, what a performance

i've only just got round to watching the film... it's good but she stands out in my eyes


### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I think honestly her performance is a good portion of why I find this movie terrifying.
2. underated actor of her generation
  - I think having an Oscar nomination for this very film means she was absolutely rated.
  - My gps told me to turn left. I said turn left to what you stupid b***h? And then I hear in her voice, Turn left? Turn left?
    - Glad I'm not the only one who does this. Also Rodney Dangerfield's lines from that film live rent free in my head.
3. She put out a great album too.
4. I remember the thumb scene - creepy.
5. Just watched this.



'WIPE THAT SMILE OFF YOUR FACE!' was such a powerful moment, really felt for her and was disgusted at how out of the loop he kept her at the threat she specifically was facing. Holy shit the Dad sucked.
6. Beside the point, but this is probably one of my least favorite Scorsese movies. It’s a good movie, but not a lot of rewatch value, and definitely forgettable compared to Taxi Driver, Raging Bull, Silence, etc. 

That scene with De Niro in the theater smoking a cigar and cackling is immortal tho.
  - problem child playing... what a film!

## 3. Who is this guy? Or what movie is this?

**Score:** 147
**Body:** Heyy guys, I’ve been searching for a very long time to find out who this guy is and where this scene comes from. I’ve looked through countless movies, clips, and forums online but still haven’t found any clear answers. If anyone recognizes this scene or has even a small clue about it, please let me know because it’s been bothering me for weeks.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. That’s the lead singer of Loaded Diaper.  Great band.
  - You mean Löded Diper?
  - The Drummer and co-lead singer
2. One of the Diary Of A Wimpy Kid movies.
3. Devon Bostick from Diary of a Wimpy Kid
4. The 100 tv series
  - Good one! Was a great tv series too.
5. Rodrik Rules!
6. Jasper from the 100.
7. The drummer of Löded Diper..

## 4. Demi Moore and her best films?

**Score:** 63
**Body:** Demi Moore Is outstanding in The Substance. She more then deserves an Oscar nod and honestly, she might just take a win. ✨ Highly recommend watching this one.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. How does indecent proposal not make the list!
  - How does GI Jane not make the list?
2. GI Jane
  - Surprised this didn’t make OP’s list, I think it’s one of her best.
3. Her best body of work was Strip Tease.
4. ghost
  - That movie was an absolute phenomenon when it came out.  It was definitely her biggest movie as far as peak fame goes.

It’s also a pretty great flick overall.
5. She did get a nod and lost.
6. No GI Jane?
7. I'd say The Substance, Ghost and A Few Good Men are my favorite of her movies.

## 5. Great performances in television series

**Score:** 51
**Body:** Steve Buschemi is definitely unforgettable in this one. My personal view on the series:
https://thefilmproject.org/movie/Boardwalk%20Empire%20(2010)#review-1a35486f-2f18-40b4-bb77-b12c70b108e2

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Boardwalk Empire is criminally underrated
2. Danielle Brooks in Orange Is The New Black. Look, OITNB has one of the most stacked ensemble casts in tv history, but Tastee stands out even among all that talent.  

Her performance in the last two seasons is incredible. But everyone in that show fires in all cylinders. Amazing cast.
3. Think this is a good series for me and my mom to watch together?
  - No, i mean yes it is a great series but you should not watch with your mom
  - Avoid this and Bates Motel as a series to watch with mother
4. The GOAT James Gandolfini as Tony Soprano.
5. Timothy Olyphant - Raylan Givens
6. Basically everyone in Deadwood not named Timothy Olyphant.
7. Donny, please...

# Top 5 posts from r/TrueFilm day

## 1. Obaltan (1961) and the Anatomy of Defeat: A Masterpiece of Korean Post-War Realism

**Score:** 12
**Body:** Revisiting **Obaltan** *(Aimless Bullet)*, I’m struck by how Yoo Hyun-mok fuses Italian Neorealism with a distinctly Korean sense of moral paralysis, creating a portrait of post-war despair that still feels uncomfortably present! The film’s cramped interiors, handheld street scenes, and jarring cuts trap the viewer inside the same psychological claustrophobia that consumes its characters. Rather than depicting dramatic collapse, Obaltan shows a slow erosion—lives quietly worn down by poverty, trauma, and a social order struggling to rebuild on spiritual ruins. Its bleakness isn’t decorative; it functions as a diagnosis, an autopsy of a society trying to move forward while still bleeding internally. What fascinates me is how the aesthetic mix of documentary immediacy and expressionist anxiety makes even brief moments of hope feel intrusive, almost inappropriate. I’m curious how contemporary viewers interpret this relentless pessimism today—does it still feel urgent, or has its emotional register shifted with time?

### Top 0 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)


## 2. Help me find this movie (for Greek weird wave lovers)

**Score:** 10
**Body:** Hi, true film lovers! Can anyone help me find the movie Arcadia (2024)? I thought I'd watched all Greek weird wave movies, but I somehow missed this one. This one particularly interests me as someone compared it to Kynodontas and Miss Violence, which are basically my two favorite movies of all time. I looked everywhere but can't seem to find it. (I'm from Eastern Europe, so keep that in mind given that some platforms are only available in the US). Thanks in advance! 

### Top 2 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Beta Cinema grabbed the worldwide distribution rights right before the Berlinale last year, but haven't released it wide yet anywhere.

It's only had festival play so far, Palm Springs in January of this year and Bari in March. The only trailer from Beta Cinema for the movie just states it's "Coming Soon", with no specific date.

So it's not available anywhere right now, unless you maybe get lucky with festival screenings. Just at a glance, another Beta Cinema release, *Aferim* (a Romanian film, so it seems comparable), took a bit over a year to go from distribution deal to showing up on streaming. Give it some time.
  - Wow, those are very detailed information, I had no idea! Thank you for taking your time to reply. Just curious, have you seen it?
2. On another note, for small filmmakers, there's always the opportunity to just write the director or someone from the production crew. I've had luck before while researching some local German film productions, and they would just send me their movies for free. Artists are usually happy to share their art when they have the option to do so.
  - Awesome idea, never would have thought of that. Thanks!

## 3. Question about ending of Day of the Beast (1995)

**Score:** 4
**Body:** So I enjoyed the movie overall but I was a little confused about the ending. They track down the anti-christ baby to the Gate of Europe, but then the racist gang are the ones responsible for killing the baby even though its shortly revealed afterwards they were ruled by Satan. So why would they be the ones to kill the antichrist? Is the intended reading that they're so racist they kill the very thing they're trying to bring about?  

### Top 0 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)


## 4. Casual Discussion Thread (November 30, 2025)

**Score:** 1
**Body:** General Discussion threads threads are meant for more casual chat; a  place to break most of the frontpage rules. Feel free to ask for  recommendations, lists, homework help; plug your site or video essay;  discuss tv here, or any such thing.

**There is no 180-character minimum for top-level comments in this thread.**

**Follow us on:**

* [Letterboxd](http://letterboxd.com/truefilmreddit/)
* [Twitter](https://twitter.com/truefilmreddit)

The sidebar has a wealth of information, including the subreddit  rules, our killer wiki, all of our projects... If you're on a mobile  app, click the "(i)" button on our frontpage.

Sincerely,

David

### Top 0 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)


## 5. Blomkamp needs another shot

**Score:** 0
**Body:** I had to pause District 9 as I was watching it, I was thrilled during the final mech fight scene. Invested so fully and realizing the stakes. Edge of my seat; if we're gonna get lame about it. I had to stop myself and the movie at that moment and realize there's been nothing quite like this since then and Blomkamp wasn't some schmuck.

Elysium came and went and no one really seemed to care much about it. I thought it was tightly constructed, had a great villain and a pulsive narrative that kept me excited. I'm biased on this one, I like it and I think it's overlooked. But again, I don't think there's any denying the energy of the directing.

Then we come to Chappie, which was a truly odd screw up of a movie. Everyone seemed to get makeup done by different studios and show up to shoot this movie at different times. It is awkward and Die Antwoord are truly terrible. The sad thing is, Copley gives a strong vocal performance and there is a shell of a really great movie here but it's fumbled by baffling decisions.

My opinion is that he deserves at least one more shot. When he was up for bat for the Alien and Robocop franchises, I was I excited! Specifically because they aren't based on his homegrown ideas. Elysium was diet-Halo and Chappie was....just not great, both of those are pretty much based off his written ideas. District 9 was more of a leftover idea from the defunct Halo film, COMBINED with Blomkamp's vague futurism ideas.

To make a long story short; I think he's a great director and a lousy writer. In that sense, I almost thought of him as the next Ridley Scott. Think there's lots of wasted potential with him.

### Top 3 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I'm going to disagree with you and say that Chappie is fantastic.  A great oddball comedy with some unique and exciting action sequences.

I've been one of the few people championing Blomkamp, but after Demonic and Gran Turismo and the shorts, I'm just not all that interested in what he has lined up next.  Too much disappointment.  I give up.
2. why are you talking about him like his career ended after chappie? he made two movies after that, and they’re totally unremarkable. he’s working on a starship troopers adaptation. he’s wasting his own potential by being a mediocre director with bad taste. 
  - His career was not the same after the failure of Chappie, full stop.
    - okay? but he’s had exactly the chances you’re saying he should have and has done basically nothing with them. he made an IP movie, someone else’s idea. and it was mediocre. and he’s getting another one. seems like it’s what you want. 
3. You know sometimes with art I'm like "why can't they just have one real banger?" 


Like it is slightly screwy that when we love something we want more of it, not realising that of course you are playing with some fire when you are making that bargain. I mean sometimes people just have one banger in them.


Though of course there are many that seem to have multiple, but they are quite rare and we really shouldn't compare every artists to those ones.

---
